_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model monitoring requirements, including data drift detection, prediction drift, performance degradation metrics, and the alerting thresholds and retraining triggers appropriate for different model types and serving patterns.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Knowledge AI-K-024 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-017 (AI, cosine: 0.450), DQ-K-004 (DQ, cosine: 0.430).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.534). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. NICE-only terms [availability, measures, system] vs WIDAI-only terms [alerting, appropriate, data, degradation, detection, different] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.914, R=0.849). P exceeds R by 0.065 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (94.8%). "Knowledge of system availability measures" and "Knowledge of model monitoring requirements, including data drift detection, pred" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.451 < STS 0.534 (gap -0.083). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [availability, measures, system] and [alerting, appropriate, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. NICE-only: [availability, measures, system]. WIDAI-only: [alerting, appropriate, data, degradation, detection, different].
